topbar.line_1: structured professional programmes in protocol, diplomacy, institutional communication, Professional English, and executive readiness.

hero.eyebrow: Programmes • Certificates • Membership • Institutional Training • WPA Card
hero.title: Structured professional programmes in protocol, diplomacy, institutional communication, Professional English, and executive readiness.
hero.subtitle: World Protocol Academy offers structured non-degree professional education for individuals, institutions, diplomatic and protocol environments, public and executive roles, and serious participants who seek more than a short course. WPA programmes are built as one coherent academic and professional scale: Foundation, Professional, Advanced, and Train-the-Trainer / Consultant.

cards.title: Levels
cards.text: The levels show the progression of learning: from basic professional readiness to advanced institutional maturity and training capability.

cards.title: Programme Families
cards.text: The families show the main thematic pillars: protocol, diplomacy, communication, etiquette, Professional English, and security-aware readiness.

cards.title: Tracks
cards.text: Tracks are practical pathways for specific audiences: protocol professionals, institutions, diplomacy, PR, public presence, security, and trainers.

cards.title: Formats
cards.text: Formats show the mode of delivery: self-paced, live online, in-person intensives, and institutional groups.

formula.title: The Central WPA Formula
formula.text: World Protocol Academy combines theory with practice — academic knowledge with applied institutional readiness, professional discipline with real public and diplomatic performance.

items.title: Programme Identity
items.text: Each programme has a clear title, level, purpose, audience, and professional outcome.

items.title: Audience logic
items.text: Each programme is created for a specific audience: institutions, embassies, protocol services, PR professionals, executives, students, or future trainers.

items.title: Learning outcomes
items.text: WPA programmes lead not only to knowledge, but to application, assessment, professional conduct, and executive confidence.

items.title: Modular Architecture
items.text: Each programme is divided into modules with a clear sequence, weight, and growth in complexity.

items.title: Lesson design
items.text: Each module contains a core lesson, applied explanation, a case or scenario, a practical task, and a mini assessment.

items.title: Exercise logic
items.text: WPA uses knowledge exercises, scenarios, correction tasks, protocol response logic, and communication drills.

items.title: Testing structure
items.text: Assessment is built into the programme: module quizzes, mid-tests, final tests, practical tasks, and scenario assessment.

items.title: Scenario depth
items.text: Scenarios grow by level: from basic to full institutional and trainer-level analysis.

items.title: Certification logic
items.text: Certificates are tied to completion, pass threshold, scenario performance, and future serial / digital verification.

items.title: Delivery and operational logic
items.text: Each WPA programme is designed to work across multiple formats without losing the discipline of the content.

cards.title: Ministries and Public Institutions
cards.text: For officials, managers, sector teams, and cabinets that work with official communication, events, visits, and public representation.

cards.title: Embassies and Diplomatic Missions
cards.text: For diplomatic and administrative environments that need protocol, communication, and representational discipline.

cards.title: Protocol and Cabinet Teams
cards.text: For people who coordinate receptions, seating, order, visits, ceremonies, speakers, and high-protocol environments.

cards.title: PR and Communication Professionals
cards.text: For people who work with messaging, public presence, institutional tone, public speaking, media, and official representation.

cards.title: Executive and Public Roles
cards.text: For executives, public officials, speakers, hosts, moderators, and front-facing professionals.

cards.title: Students and Future Trainers
cards.text: For serious participants who want to develop systematically and gradually enter the WPA scale, including the Train-the-Trainer pathway.

cards.title: Foundation Certificate Programme
cards.text: Entry-level training in protocol, etiquette, bon ton, first impression, formal conduct, professional courtesy, and basic institutional orientation.

cards.title: Professional Certificate Programme
cards.text: Intermediate professional level for state and diplomatic protocol, official visits, ceremonies, representation, communication, and practical protocol application in real environments.

cards.title: Advanced Certificate Programme
cards.text: Advanced level for security-aware protocol work, multilateral environments, complex institutional situations, coordination, and advanced professional judgement.

cards.title: Train-the-Trainer / Consultant Programme
cards.text: Specialised level for future trainers, facilitators, consultants, and holders of knowledge in protocol, communication, and institutional training.

cards.title: 1. Core Lesson
cards.text: The key concept, rule, standard, or professional framework.

cards.title: 2. Applied Explanation
cards.text: How that concept functions in a real professional context.

cards.title: 3. Case or Scenario
cards.text: A specific situation for reading, analysis, and judgement.

cards.title: 4. Practical Task
cards.text: A correction, briefing, response, speaking, writing, or protocol logic exercise.

cards.title: 5. Mini Assessment
cards.text: A short quiz or evaluation that checks real readiness.

cards.title: Protocol, Diplomacy, and Institutional Order
items: Order of precedence, seating, flags, and symbols

cards.title: Etiquette, Bon Ton, and Professional Conduct
Protocol Professional Track
cards.text: For protocol units, public institutions, executive offices, and professionals who work with formal responsibilities and representation.

cards.title: Communication and Presence Track
cards.text: For PR professionals, speakers, public figures, and people who must communicate clearly, with control, and with credibility.

cards.title: Etiquette and Bon Ton Track
cards.text: For a broader audience seeking personal polish, social intelligence, business courtesy, and a stable public impression.

cards.title: Institutional and Diplomatic Track
cards.text: For ministries, embassies, offices for international cooperation, and diplomatic or multilateral environments.

cards.title: Advanced Security-Aware Track
cards.text: For people who work where protocol meets movement, sensitivity, discretion, and high-level coordination.

cards.title: Trainer and Consultant Track
cards.text: For future trainers, educators, facilitators, and consultants who want to deliver the WPA standard.

cards.title: Foundation Assessment
items: Focus: basic readiness and professional conduct

threshold.title: Threshold Logic
threshold.html: Recommended pass threshold: 70%. Distinction: 85%+. High distinction: 92%+. In the future WPA architecture, each certificate should carry a programme title, level, score band, serial number, and digital verification path.

cards.title: Foundation Certificate in Protocol and Professional Conduct
cards.text: An entry certificate for basic readiness in protocol, etiquette, bon ton, and formal public impression.

details.label: Core outcome
details.body: Basic protocol literacy, professional courtesy, and initial institutional confidence.

details.label: Assessment
details.body: 8 quizzes, 1 mid-test, 1 final test, and 1 short scenario.

details.label: Format
details.body: Self-paced, live online, in person, or institutional group.

cards.title: Professional Certificate in State and Diplomatic Protocol
cards.text: An applied certificate for protocol professionals, institutional environments, official visits, and ceremonies.

details.label: Core outcome
details.body: Capability in state and diplomatic protocol, official events, ceremonial coordination, and formal communication.

details.label: Assessment
details.body: 10 quizzes, 1 mid-test, 1 final test, 2 scenarios, and 1 correction task.

details.label: Format
details.body: Professional group, institutional format, or guided digital pathway.

cards.title: Advanced Certificate in Protocol, Security and Institutional Readiness
cards.text: An advanced certificate for complex institutional and security-aware environments, multilateral readiness, and high-level judgement.

details.label: Core outcome
details.body: Strategic readiness, confident conduct, coordination awareness, and sensitive formal judgement.

details.label: Assessment
details.body: 10 quizzes, 1 mid-test, 1 final test, 3 scenarios, and 1 briefing task.

details.label: Format
details.body: Advanced group, executive delivery, or institutional format.

cards.title: Train-the-Trainer / Consultant Certificate
cards.text: A specialised certificate for future trainers, facilitators, consultants, and holders of structured WPA knowledge.

details.label: Core outcome
details.body: Capability for professional teaching, ethical delivery, scenario facilitation, and assessment design.

details.label: Assessment
details.body: 8 quizzes, 1 final theory test, 2 teaching scenarios, 1 micro-teaching, and 1 design task.

details.label: Format
details.body: Trainer cohort, consultancy pathway, or institutional instructor preparation.

verification.title: Certificate + QR + verification logic
verification.text: In the future WPA structure, each certificate should be tied to serial verification, QR verification, programme traceability, and a clear status in the WPA Card ecosystem.

cards.title: Free
cards.text: Basic access to introductory content, preview of learning paths, short bot answers, and basic orientation.

cards.title: WPA Pro
cards.text: Deeper answers, premium lessons, practice scenarios, and clearer professional direction.

cards.title: Academic Pro
cards.text: For students, researchers, and serious academic preparation with more advanced WPAWS logic and certificate preparation support.

cards.title: Institutional / Executive
cards.text: For ministries, embassies, universities, organisations, and executive teams with custom delivery and group formats.

path.title: The Ideal WPA Path
path.html: Free → Pro / Academic Pro → Certificate Programme → WPA Card → next level → Institutional or Trainer pathway

cards.title: WPA Free Card
cards.text: A basic digital identification layer for a free user and a first entry into the WPA ecosystem.

cards.title: WPA Pro Card
cards.text: For active members who use premium content, deeper answers, and structured access.

cards.title: WPA Academic Pro Card
cards.text: For students, researchers, and serious academic users with more advanced verification and learning-path logic.

cards.title: WPA Institutional / Executive Card
cards.text: For institutions, teams, trainers, executives, and partner layers.

logic.title: Card logic
logic.text: In phase two, the WPA Card should be linked to QR identification, certificate verification, member status, future partner benefits, and institutional access control. QR is the first and most rational step.

rows.html: Opening and review — goals, brief context, and a review of the previous session.
Core lesson — doctrine, rules, concepts, and structured explanation.
Short break — reset and preparation for applied work.
Applied explanation — worked examples, demonstrations, and practical interpretation.
Case or scenario — reading, professional diagnosis, and response logic.
Practical task — correction, briefing, response, writing, or protocol logic task.
Quiz, reflection, and debrief — short assessment, synthesis, and next steps.

cards.title: Self-paced
cards.text: Independent learning with clear structure, quizzes, scenarios, reading logic, and future AI-supported review.

cards.title: Live Online
cards.text: Structured online sessions, cohort rhythm, feedback, and real guided teaching interaction.

cards.title: In-person Intensives
cards.text: Short, dense, premium programmes with practical work, simulations, formal drills, and executive-style training.

cards.title: Institutional Groups
cards.text: Adapted formats for ministries, embassies, universities, public institutions, companies, and professional teams.

box.title: What this means in practice
box.text: WPA does not present itself as a state diplomatic academy and does not claim to award university degrees. Its strength lies in structure, application, certification, sources, professional relevance, and digital architecture.

cta.title: Choose your WPA path
cta.text: Whether you are starting independently, preparing for a public or institutional role, working in a protocol or diplomatic environment, or building a trainer pathway — WPA has a clear entry point and a clear next step.

footer.brand_text: An independent digital academy and professional platform for protocol, diplomacy, institutional communication, Professional English, security-aware readiness, and public representation.
footer.disclaimer: WPA offers structured non-degree professional education, certificate pathways, and institutional training. It does not claim to award university degrees.
